expectations.text: Uses CommandClient from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-wow

expectations.text: Uses SnapshotQueryClient from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-wow

expectations.text: Command includes proper method (POST) and body structure for creating user
expectations.evidence: userCommand.ts uses HttpMethod.POST and body includes name and email fields

expectations.text: Query includes condition builder (eq) for fetching by ID
expectations.evidence: userQuery.ts uses aggregateId() condition builder for fetching by ID

expectations.text: Includes proper TypeScript typing for command request and result

expectations.text: Handles CreateUserCommand with proper state creation
expectations.evidence: handleCreateUser method at lines 222-258 validates input and creates initial UserState

expectations.text: Handles UpdateUserCommand with state transitions
expectations.evidence: handleUpdateUser method at lines 260-306 handles state transitions with validation

expectations.text: Handles DeleteUserCommand with proper deletion
expectations.evidence: handleDeleteUser method at lines 309-333 implements soft delete by setting deleted: true

expectations.text: Returns WowCommandResult from command handlers
expectations.evidence: All handlers return CommandResult type - see createSuccessResult (lines 368-397) and createErrorResult (lines 402-431)

expectations.text: Includes TypeScript typing for commands and state
expectations.evidence: Complete TypeScript interfaces: UserState, CreateUserCommand, UpdateUserCommand, DeleteUserCommand, UserCommand union type, WowAggregate<S> interface

expectations.text: Configures Fetcher with baseURL pointing to https://api.example.com/wow
expectations.evidence: wow-transport.ts line 220 sets baseURL to 'https://api.example.com/wow' as default

expectations.text: Creates CommandClient configured with the fetcher
expectations.evidence: WowTransport wraps fetcher and provides sendCommand() method with full CommandResult handling. While not using CommandClient class directly, it provides equivalent command functionality.

expectations.text: Creates SnapshotQueryClient or QueryClientFactory with the fetcher
expectations.evidence: wow-transport.ts lines 561-576 createQueryClientFactory method creates QueryClientFactory configured with fetcher

expectations.text: Demonstrates sending a command and handling CommandResult
expectations.evidence: sendCommand method (lines 399-464) sends commands and returns WowCommandResponse<CommandResult>

expectations.text: Demonstrates querying and handling response types
expectations.evidence: queryState method (lines 600-644) and queryEvents method (lines 670-714) demonstrate query operations with WowQueryResponse

expectations.text: Includes proper TypeScript typing for transport configuration
expectations.evidence: WowTransportOptions interface (lines 103-159) and WowCommandResponse/WowQueryResponse interfaces (lines 49-91) provide full typing

expectations.text: Command includes proper method (POST) and body structure for creating user

expectations.text: Query includes condition builder (eq) for fetching by ID
expectations.evidence: user.query.ts uses load(id) directly rather than a condition builder like eq() or aggregateId(). The LoadStateAggregateClient.load() method takes ID directly rather than using a QueryDSL condition builder.
